---
title: "MRIQC DB Aggregator"
subtitle: "From a page-oriented Mongo API to an analysis-ready dashboard"
author: "BrainhackDC working deck"
date: 2026-04-17
format:
  revealjs:
    footer: "MRIQC DB Aggregator"
---

## Overview

::: {.hero-grid}
::: {.hero-copy}
<div class="eyebrow">Project summary</div>

<p class="lede"><strong>MRIQC DB Aggregator</strong> is the translation layer between the public MRIQC Web API and the kind of fast, dedupe-aware exploratory interface researchers actually need.</p>

<div class="tag-row">
  <span class="tag">source contract first</span>
  <span class="tag">raw payloads archived</span>
  <span class="tag">typed PostgreSQL facts</span>
  <span class="tag">dashboard-oriented API</span>
</div>

<div class="callout">
  The problem is not the existence of MongoDB. The problem is that the public interface is optimized for paged document retrieval, not repeated analytical reads.
</div>
:::

::: {.hero-panel}
<div class="panel-title">Current snapshot</div>

- MRIQC DB stores MRIQC-derived quality metrics and source metadata for `T1w`, `T2w`, and `bold`.
- The public service is page-oriented and capped at `max_results=50`.
- That makes filtering, histograms, and duplicate-aware comparison awkward to do interactively.

<div class="stat-grid">
  <div class="stat"><span class="value">231,100</span><span class="label">sampled observations</span></div>
  <div class="stat"><span class="value">676 MB</span><span class="label">raw JSON archived</span></div>
  <div class="stat"><span class="value">2017-2025</span><span class="label">archive span in sample</span></div>
</div>
:::
:::

## Migration Strategy

::: {.grid-2}
::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">Principles</div>

- Migrate against the application-level API contract, not MongoDB internals.
- Save exact page payloads first so extraction and transformation are decoupled.
- Keep the loader rerunnable from local raw payloads.
- Preserve unknown fields in JSON extras instead of forcing premature normalization.

<div class="quote-block">
  Raw first, typed second, canonical later.
</div>
:::

::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">Flow</div>

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A["MRIQC API<br/>Eve + MongoDB"] --> B["Representative page pull<br/>exact JSON saved"]
  B --> C["Typed loaders<br/>T1w / T2w / bold"]
  C --> D["PostgreSQL fact tables<br/>indexes + dedupe metadata"]
  D --> E["FastAPI read layer<br/>overview / metrics / duplicates"]
  E --> F["Dashboard"]
```

<p class="caption">This keeps parser changes cheap and preserves an audit trail back to the exact source payloads.</p>
:::
:::

## Schema, Types, and Dedupe

::: {.grid-2}
::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">Schema and type choices</div>

- Separate fact tables for `t1w`, `t2w`, and `bold`.
- Shared columns aligned so a future `UNION ALL` view is still possible.
- `source_api_id` used as the idempotent upsert key.
- Identifiers kept as strings when the source contract treats them as strings.
- Source fragments preserved in JSON extras for forward compatibility.

<div class="pill-list">
  <span class="pill">typed filters</span>
  <span class="pill">timezone-aware dates</span>
  <span class="pill">float IQMs</span>
  <span class="pill">JSON overflow columns</span>
</div>
:::

::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">Dedupe pressure</div>

<div class="metric-list">
  <div class="metric"><span class="name">T1w exact groups</span><span class="number">3,047</span></div>
  <div class="metric"><span class="name">T2w exact groups</span><span class="number">13,993</span></div>
  <div class="metric"><span class="name">bold exact groups</span><span class="number">11,380</span></div>
  <div class="metric"><span class="name">largest T1w group</span><span class="number">3,479 rows</span></div>
  <div class="metric"><span class="name">largest T2w group</span><span class="number">71 rows</span></div>
  <div class="metric"><span class="name">largest bold group</span><span class="number">76 rows</span></div>
</div>

- `dedupe_exact_key`: direct from `provenance.md5sum`
- `dedupe_series_key`: modality-local identity hash
- `raw`, `exact`, and `series` remain read semantics over the same base tables
:::
:::

## Pipeline Reproducibility and Expected Gains

::: {.grid-2}
::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">Why the pipeline is reproducible</div>

- Representative sampling is page-aware and rerunnable.
- Loads are idempotent through `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE`.
- `db-init` and `db-load` are safe to rerun after parser or schema changes.
- Unknown source fields are retained rather than dropped.

<div class="timeline">
  <div class="timeline-item"><div class="phase">Pull</div><div class="body">Archive representative raw API pages under `data/runs/<run-id>/raw/`.</div></div>
  <div class="timeline-item"><div class="phase">Load</div><div class="body">Parse into typed fact rows and compute dedupe metadata.</div></div>
  <div class="timeline-item"><div class="phase">Serve</div><div class="body">Read directly through a thin FastAPI layer while query patterns stabilize.</div></div>
</div>
:::

::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">Expected improvements</div>

- Indexed filters on manufacturer, model, version, task, and time.
- Cheap SQL aggregates for overview cards, histograms, and missingness.
- Dedupe-aware reads that do not distort reference distributions.
- Clean path to materialized views for repeated hot queries.

<div class="callout">
  The storage layer is now aligned to the dashboard workload. Benchmarking the final p50/p95 query wins comes after the frontend settles on its real query patterns.
</div>
:::
:::

## Dashboard Design I

::: {.grid-2}
::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">Design goals</div>

- Start with overview plus drilldown.
- Make `raw`, `exact`, and `series` visible everywhere.
- Keep filtering focused on a small number of stable, meaningful fields.
- Support the central researcher question: “How does this slice compare to the broader MRIQC reference set?”

<div class="tag-row">
  <span class="tag">overview cards</span>
  <span class="tag">metric explorer</span>
  <span class="tag">duplicate inspection</span>
  <span class="tag">shareable filter state</span>
</div>
:::

::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">Wireframe direction</div>

```{=html}
<div class="mini-browser">
  <div class="toolbar">
    <span class="dot"></span><span class="dot"></span><span class="dot"></span>
    <code>/overview?view=series&amp;manufacturers=Siemens</code>
  </div>
  <div class="mock-dashboard">
    <div class="mock-sidebar">
      <div class="section-title"><strong>Filters</strong><span class="badge">sticky</span></div>
      <div class="filter-line"></div>
      <div class="filter-line mid"></div>
      <div class="filter-line short"></div>
      <div class="filter-line"></div>
      <div class="section-title"><strong>View mode</strong><span class="badge">raw / exact / series</span></div>
      <div class="filter-line mid"></div>
      <div class="filter-line short"></div>
    </div>
    <div class="mock-main">
      <div class="mock-panel">
        <div class="section-title"><strong>Archive summary</strong><span class="badge">fast aggregate</span></div>
        <div class="stat-grid">
          <div class="stat"><span class="value">7,222</span><span class="label">T1w exact</span></div>
          <div class="stat"><span class="value">60,670</span><span class="label">T2w exact</span></div>
          <div class="stat"><span class="value">45,710</span><span class="label">bold exact</span></div>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="mock-row">
        <div class="mock-panel">
          <div class="section-title"><strong>Metric histogram</strong><span class="badge">same filters</span></div>
          <div class="chart-bars">
            <span></span><span></span><span></span><span></span><span></span><span></span><span></span><span></span>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div class="mock-panel">
          <div class="section-title"><strong>Top values</strong><span class="badge">scanner / version</span></div>
          <div class="list-line"></div>
          <div class="list-line mid"></div>
          <div class="list-line short"></div>
          <div class="list-line"></div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```
:::
:::

## Dashboard Design II

::: {.feature-grid}
::: {.feature-card}
<div class="panel-title">Reference comparison</div>

Support cohort-versus-reference comparisons so a study can be placed against the broader MRIQC archive, not just inspected in isolation.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
<div class="panel-title">Saved views</div>

Persist filters and view mode in shareable URLs so analyses are reproducible and easy to discuss with collaborators.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
<div class="panel-title">Serving optimizations</div>

Add materialized views and pre-aggregated histograms only where real usage demonstrates repeated expensive reads.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
<div class="panel-title">Data-quality workflows</div>

Make duplicates, missingness, and metadata drift visible so the archive is easier to interpret and trust.
:::
:::

## Dashboard Tech Stack

<div class="stack-grid">
  <div class="stack-card"><span class="stack-label">Frontend</span><h3>React + TypeScript + Vite</h3><p>Use the existing frontend scaffold as the dashboard surface for interactive exploration and comparison.</p></div>
  <div class="stack-card"><span class="stack-label">API</span><h3>FastAPI</h3><p>Expose overview, metrics, distributions, missingness, extras, and duplicate summaries through a thin read layer.</p></div>
  <div class="stack-card"><span class="stack-label">Storage</span><h3>PostgreSQL + SQLAlchemy</h3><p>Typed fact tables plus indexes and dedupe metadata make repeated analytical queries practical.</p></div>
  <div class="stack-card"><span class="stack-label">Workflow</span><h3>Pixi + Docker Compose</h3><p>Keep local setup reproducible for sampling, loading, profiling, API work, and presentation builds.</p></div>
</div>

<div class="callout">
  The presentation itself stays in a separate Quarto repo because that makes visual tuning and GitHub Pages deployment simple.
</div>

## Demo Flow

::: {.grid-2}
::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">What to show live</div>

<div class="demo-steps">
  <div class="demo-step"><div class="step-number">1</div><div><strong>Overview first.</strong> Show the modality counts and the difference between `raw`, `exact`, and `series`.</div></div>
  <div class="demo-step"><div class="step-number">2</div><div><strong>Filter a slice.</strong> Narrow by manufacturer, MRIQC version, or task and keep the query responsive.</div></div>
  <div class="demo-step"><div class="step-number">3</div><div><strong>Open a metric distribution.</strong> Reuse the same filters and compare the histogram across view modes.</div></div>
  <div class="demo-step"><div class="step-number">4</div><div><strong>Inspect duplicates.</strong> Show why dedupe is necessary before treating the archive as a benchmark distribution.</div></div>
</div>
:::

::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">Current backend surface</div>

```text
GET /api/v1/overview
GET /api/v1/modalities/{modality}/profile
GET /api/v1/modalities/{modality}/metrics
GET /api/v1/modalities/{modality}/metrics/{field_name}
GET /api/v1/modalities/{modality}/duplicates/{kind}
```

- The backend demo path is already real.
- The frontend can now be iterated against stable read semantics.
- This slide can also act as a fallback if a full live demo is not ready.
:::
:::

## Future Directions

::: {.closing}
::: {.hero-copy}
<div class="eyebrow">Next phase</div>

<p class="lede"><strong>Next up:</strong> make the dashboard genuinely useful, then optimize the serving layer based on actual usage instead of guesswork.</p>

<div class="roadmap">
  <div class="roadmap-item"><h3>Normalize carefully</h3><p>Introduce subject, session, and scanner dimensions without inventing a fake universal scan key.</p></div>
  <div class="roadmap-item"><h3>Benchmark reads</h3><p>Measure p50 and p95 query latency once the frontend settles on real interaction patterns.</p></div>
  <div class="roadmap-item"><h3>Strengthen comparisons</h3><p>Support cohort-versus-reference workflows that help researchers interpret their own MRIQC outputs.</p></div>
  <div class="roadmap-item"><h3>Operationalize sharing</h3><p>Decide where saved views, publishing, and auth controls add value once the dashboard exists.</p></div>
</div>
:::

::: {.hero-panel}
<div class="panel-title">Takeaway</div>

- Preserve source fidelity first.
- Make ingestion rerunnable.
- Keep dedupe explicit.
- Shape storage around the dashboard workload.
- Optimize only after the interface proves where the hot paths are.
:::
:::
